message:
scripts+ci: smoke-all auto-detects mcp-server, runs --live when present

Iter 38's smoke-all.sh was running ui/scripts/smoke.sh without
--live, so the live-mcp-server coverage (INIT_OK + LIST_COLLECTIONS_OK
audit codes, the iter 34 8-tool count assertion) was silently absent
from the all-smokes flow.

Fix:
  - smoke-all.sh checks for mcp-server/target/release/rulake-mcp at
    startup. If present → smoke.sh --live with label "Console
    WASM-local + live mcp-server". If absent → smoke.sh with label
    "Console WASM-local (no live; build mcp-server first for --live)"
  - inner runner now uses bash -c to word-split the command so --live
    reaches the inner script as a separate argv (not part of the path)
  - CI smoke job builds mcp-server + mcp-rvdna binaries before
    running scripts/smoke-all.sh, so the auto-detect picks up --live
    in CI without a flag

Verified locally:
  ✓ Console WASM-local + live mcp-server         38s
  ✓ rvdna-mcp HTTP                                2s
  ✓ Console + mcp-rvdna cross-component          19s
  ✓ smoke-all green — every script passed

CI now exercises Console+mcp-server live mode + Console+mcp-rvdna
cross-component on every PR. The iter 34 default-capability gap and
the iter 32 cross-component bug class are both regression-locked at
the workflow level.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -114,6 +114,9 @@ jobs:
       - uses: browser-actions/setup-chrome@v1
       - name: Pre-install agent-browser (warms npx cache)
         run: npx --yes agent-browser install || true
+      - name: Build mcp-server binary (enables smoke-all auto --live)
+        working-directory: mcp-server
+        run: cargo build --release --bin rulake-mcp
       - name: Build mcp-rvdna binary
         working-directory: mcp-rvdna
         run: cargo build --release --bin rvdna-mcp

@@ -40,9 +40,22 @@ for arg in "$@"; do
   esac
 done
 
+# Auto-detect mcp-server binary so the Console smoke runs in --live
+# mode (which adds INIT_OK + LIST_COLLECTIONS_OK audit codes and the
+# live tool-count assertion against mcp-server's 8 tools). If the
+# binary isn't built yet, fall back silently to WASM-local-only.
+MCP_BIN="${REPO_DIR}/mcp-server/target/release/rulake-mcp"
+if [[ -x "${MCP_BIN}" ]]; then
+  CONSOLE_SMOKE="${REPO_DIR}/ui/scripts/smoke.sh --live"
+  CONSOLE_LABEL="Console WASM-local + live mcp-server"
+else
+  CONSOLE_SMOKE="${REPO_DIR}/ui/scripts/smoke.sh"
+  CONSOLE_LABEL="Console WASM-local (no live; build mcp-server first for --live)"
+fi
+
 # Smoke registry — pairs of (label, command). Add new smokes here.
 SMOKES=(
-  "Console WASM-local|${REPO_DIR}/ui/scripts/smoke.sh"
+  "${CONSOLE_LABEL}|${CONSOLE_SMOKE}"
   "rvdna-mcp HTTP|${REPO_DIR}/mcp-rvdna/scripts/http-smoke.sh"
 )
 if [[ "${SKIP_CROSS}" -eq 0 ]]; then
@@ -58,7 +71,12 @@ for entry in "${SMOKES[@]}"; do
   printf '    %s\n\n' "${cmd}"
 
   START=$(date +%s)
-  "${cmd}"
+  # Word-split cmd so flags like `--live` reach the inner script as
+  # separate argv entries (not part of the path). The smoke-script
+  # paths are repo-internal and operator-controlled, so word-splitting
+  # is safe here.
+  # shellcheck disable=SC2086
+  bash -c "${cmd}"
   ec=$?
   ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START))
